_comment: Model-calibration constants (UX_APP_DEV_SPEC.md 4.C/10; schema v2 per PLAN_PARTLOAD_CALIBRATION.md 4.0). All null/absent fit fields = LEGACY behaviour, so this schema lands BEFORE the fits. thr_sigma.points / icv.sigma / exvanos_base.surface are written by scripts/fit_partload.py (atomic tmp+replace, with fit_meta provenance); mouth_rad.alpha and exvanos_base.wot_base_stable are re-fitted in Phase 3 on the measured geometry. Env vars (OPENWAM_MOUTH_RAD/_W, OPENWAM_EXVANOS_BASE/_SCALE, OPENWAM_THR_SIGMA_BP, OPENWAM_ICV_SIGMA) still override at runtime for studies.

thr_sigma._comment: Calibrated pedal->sigma breakpoints (Phase 4B). Lives HERE, not in ThrottleConfig: it is a fitted, run-traceable, foldable calibration curve (ThrottleConfig keeps only the physical offset/gamma). Anchors: 0.0->0.001 and 1.0->0.96 keep the closed/WOT terminations unchanged. enabled=false or null sigmas -> geometric legacy path.

icv._comment: Fitted ICV effective open-area ratio (Phase 4A; rail model only). null -> SimConfig intake.eq_tube.icv_sigma default. Physically the ICV is ECU duty-cycle controlled (risk R3): we fit ONE constant for loads >= 10% and do NOT table it.

exvanos_base._comment: WOT: per-rpm points (Phase-3 measured-geometry fit, fit_cam_deg 260, use_shape_fit=true). wot_base_stable is the flat fallback datum (set use_shape_fit=false to fold). Part load: surface (Phase 4C) or part_load_const. is_wot short-circuits to the WOT table, so the WOT row cannot regress from part-load fitting. +-5 pert map at the fitted bases: 6300/6900 smooth (<=3.3pp); 5300 -5 smooth, +5 dips -15pp (converged); 2700/3900 sit on sharp lever-ceiling PEAKS (+-5 loses 7-20pp, converged, no collapse). Hard cliffs: 3900@b150 and 6900@b177 blow up. See EXHAUST_STABILIZATION_NOTES Stage 57.

fit_meta.note: Measured-geometry rail WOT fit (alpha 0.4, rail/tap fric 0.1). BEST-ACHIEVABLE points: fitted row [87.5, 91.8, 116.6, 112.6, 110.7, 109.8] vs stock [103.7, 115.7, 111.3, 110.1, 108.7, 106.0] -> r 0.05, max_norm_shape_err 0.176, peak 5300 vs stock 3900: the r>=0.95 gate is NOT met. 2700/3900 are base-lever CEILINGS (b130; lower bases REVERSE), 6900 is a floor (b177 blows up), i.e. the low-rpm charge deficit of the measured geometry is outside the EXVANOS lever range. Ratio-wise 4600-6900 sit at 1.02-1.05 of stock (legacy datum was 0.79-0.87 everywhere). Part-load calibration is insulated by the p=VE/VE_WOT normalization. Header-length hypothesis falsified (650mm primaries: 2700 -5.9pp, 6900 blow-up).
